title: Add Areas of Composite Parts
description: Find the total area of a composite figure by summing the areas of its non-overlapping parts.
condition: Areas of multiple non-overlapping regions of a figure are known.
response: Add the individual areas to obtain the total area.
label: Principle
example: If a figure is composed of a rectangle with area $52.5$ and two triangles each with area $5.25$, then total area $= 52.5 + 5.25 + 5.25 = 63$.

title: Area of a Parallelogram
description: Calculate the area of a parallelogram by multiplying its base by its corresponding height.
condition: Given a parallelogram with a specified base and its perpendicular height.
response: Compute area as base × height.
label: Principle / Model
example: For base $18$ cm and height $21.6$ cm, area $=18\times21.6=388.8\text{ cm}^2$.
notes: Height must be perpendicular to the chosen base.

title: Area of a Rectangle
description: Compute the area of a rectangle using length multiplied by width.
condition: A rectangle with given length $l$ and width $w$.
response: Compute $A = l \times w$.
label: Principle
example: If $l=12$ and $w=3\frac{1}{4}$, then $A = 12 \times 3\frac{1}{4} = 39$.
notes: — [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Area of a Trapezoid
description: Compute the area of a trapezoid using the average of the bases times the height.
condition: A trapezoid with bases $b_1, b_2$ and height $h$.
response: Compute $A = \tfrac{1}{2}(b_1 + b_2)h$.
label: Principle
notes: — [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Area of a Triangle
description: Compute the area of a triangle using one-half the product of base and height.
condition: A triangle with base $b$ and corresponding height $h$.
response: Compute $A = \tfrac{1}{2}bh$.
label: Principle
notes: — [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Combine Adjacent Segment Lengths
description: Find a full base length by adding adjacent collinear segment lengths.
condition: Given two or more adjacent segments that form a single straight base.
response: Add the segment lengths to obtain the total base length.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a base is split into $16$ in and $8$ in, total base $=16+8=24$ in.
notes: Segments must be collinear parts of the same base.

title: Compute Lateral Area of a Rectangular Prism
description: Find the total area of the four side faces of a rectangular prism by summing the areas of the rectangular sides (excluding top and bottom).
condition: Given a right rectangular prism with length l, width w, and height h, and asked for the area of the sides only (not top or bottom).
response: Compute 2lh + 2wh.
label: Principle / Model
example: For a prism with $l=36$, $w=24$, $h=8$, lateral area $=2(36)(8)+2(24)(8)=576+384=960$ square inches.
notes: Used in Part A to find painted blue area.

title: Compute Paint Required from Coverage Rate
description: Determine the amount of paint needed by dividing total area by the coverage per unit and rounding as required.
condition: Given total area A and coverage rate c (area per unit), and asked for number of units needed.
response: Compute A ÷ c and round to the specified place value.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $A=960$ sq in and $c=125$ sq in/oz, then $960 \div 125 = 7.68 \approx 7.7$ ounces.
notes: Includes rounding to nearest tenth.

title: Compute Surface Area of a Composite Solid
description: Add the areas of all exterior faces of a solid composed of multiple prisms or pyramids, ensuring no internal faces are included.
condition: Given a composite solid (e.g., rectangular prism attached to triangular prism) with dimensions.
response: Sum the areas of all exterior rectangular and triangular faces using appropriate area formulas.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires excluding shared interior faces.

title: Compute Surface Area of a Square Pyramid from Net
description: Find total surface area by adding the area of the square base and four congruent triangular faces.
condition: Given a net of a square pyramid with base side length s and triangular face height h.
response: Compute $s^2 + 4\left(\tfrac{1}{2}sh\right)$.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses area formulas for squares and triangles.

title: Compute Volume of a Cube
description: Calculate the volume of a cube by cubing the side length.
condition: Given the side length of a cube.
response: Compute side × side × side (or side³).
label: Principle / Model
example: If side length is $\tfrac{2}{3}$ ft, then $V = \left(\tfrac{2}{3}\right)^3 = \tfrac{8}{27}$ ft³.
notes: Special case of prism volume.

title: Count Unit Cubes by Volume Ratio
description: Determine how many smaller cubes fit in a prism by dividing total volume by volume of one cube.
condition: Volume of a large prism and edge length of a smaller cube are known.
response: Compute number of cubes as $\dfrac{V_{\text{prism}}}{V_{\text{cube}}}$.
label: Principle
example: If prism volume $=156.75$ in$^3$ and cube volume $=(\tfrac{1}{4})^3=\tfrac{1}{64}$, then number $=156.75 \div \tfrac{1}{64}=10032$.
notes: Uses division by a fraction.

title: Count Units on Coordinate Plane
description: Determine horizontal or vertical side lengths by subtracting coordinate values.
condition: Two points share the same x- or y-coordinate on a grid.
response: Find side length by taking the absolute difference of the other coordinate values.
label: Principle / Model
example: Distance between $(1,5)$ and $(1,-4)$ is $|5-(-4)|=9$.

title: Decompose Composite Figure
description: Break a complex figure into simpler shapes (rectangles and/or triangles) to compute total area.
condition: A composite planar figure with identifiable simpler subregions.
response: Partition into known shapes, compute each area, and combine appropriately (add or subtract).
label: Principle / Model
example: A hexagon inside a $20 \times 20$ square can be found by $400 - 4\left(\frac{1}{2}(4)(10)\right)$.

title: Divide by Unit Rate
description: Determine the number of groups by dividing total quantity by the amount per group.
condition: Total amount and per-unit amount are given.
response: Compute total ÷ per-unit amount.
label: Principle
example: If total area is $63$ m$^2$ and each roll covers $7$ m$^2$, then rolls needed $=63 \div 7 = 9$.
notes: May require rounding up in other contexts ???

title: Divide Total Area by Unit Area
description: Find the number of equal-sized units needed to cover a region by dividing total area by the area of one unit.
condition: Total area of a region and area covered by one unit are given.
response: Compute number of units as $\frac{\text{total area}}{\text{area per unit}}$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If total area is $7.5$ ft$^2$ and each brick covers $0.25$ ft$^2$, then $7.5 \div 0.25 = 30$ bricks.

title: Double Area for Two Identical Faces
description: When two congruent faces are included (e.g., top and bottom), multiply one face’s area by 2.
condition: Given area of one face and asked for total area of two identical faces.
response: Multiply the single-face area by 2.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one face is $157.5$ in², then total for two faces is $2 \times 157.5 = 315$ in².
notes: Requires recognizing congruent faces.

title: Horizontal or Vertical Distance on Coordinate Plane
description: Find the distance between two points that share either the same x- or same y-coordinate.
condition: Two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) where x1 = x2 or y1 = y2.
response: Compute distance as the absolute difference of the differing coordinates: $|x_2 - x_1|$ or $|y_2 - y_1|$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Base and Height from Coordinates
description: Determine a valid base and corresponding perpendicular height for a polygon using coordinate differences.
condition: Vertices of a polygon given on the coordinate plane.
response: Select horizontal or vertical segment as base and use perpendicular coordinate difference as height.
label: Principle / Model
example: For $X(-2,-5)$ and $Y(2,-5)$, base $=4$; height from $Z(0,7)$ to $y=-5$ is $12$.

title: Match 3D Figure to Net
description: Identify which two-dimensional net folds to form a given three-dimensional figure.
condition: A 3D solid and several candidate nets are shown.
response: Select the net whose faces and arrangement correspond to the solid.
label: Category
example: A cube matches a net of six congruent squares arranged in a cross shape.
notes: Relies on spatial visualization. [merged: response varies]

title: Multiply Area by Unit Cost
description: Determine total cost by multiplying area by the cost per unit area.
condition: Given an area measurement and a cost per square unit.
response: Compute total cost as area × cost per unit area.
label: Principle / Model
example: If area $=75\text{ ft}^2$ and cost $=\$4.50$ per ft$^2$, total cost $=75\times4.50=\$337.50$.
notes: Ensure units match (e.g., square feet with dollars per square foot).

title: Perimeter by Adding Side Lengths
description: Find the perimeter of a polygon by summing the lengths of all its sides.
condition: Side lengths of a polygon are known or can be determined.
response: Add all side lengths to compute the perimeter.
label: Principle / Model
example: If side lengths are $9,7,4,3,5,4$, then perimeter $=9+7+4+3+5+4=32$.

title: Recognize Valid Cube Net
description: Determine whether a given arrangement of six connected squares can be folded to form a cube.
condition: Given a figure composed of six connected congruent squares.
response: State Yes if it folds into a cube without overlap; otherwise state No.
label: Category
example: A cross-shaped arrangement of six squares with four in a row and one above and below the second square is a valid cube net (Yes).
notes: Classification based on known cube nets.

title: Solve for Base from Triangle Area
description: Determine a triangle’s base by solving the area formula for the unknown base.
condition: Given the area of a triangle and its height, with base unknown.
response: Set up $A=\frac{1}{2}bh$ and solve algebraically for $b$.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given $10\tfrac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{2}b\left(2\tfrac{5}{8}\right)$, solve to get $b=8$.
notes: Requires isolating the variable using inverse operations.

title: Solve for Missing Dimension Using Volume
description: Find an unknown dimension of a rectangular prism by dividing volume by the product of the known dimensions.
condition: Volume $V$ and two dimensions $l,w$ of a rectangular prism are known; third dimension unknown.
response: Compute missing dimension as $\dfrac{V}{lw}$.
label: Principle
notes: Requires correct multiplication of mixed numbers.

title: Solve for Missing Height Using Area Formula
description: Given the area of a triangle and one dimension, solve for the unknown height.
condition: Triangle area A and base b known, height h unknown.
response: Use $A = \frac{1}{2}bh$ and solve for $h$.
label: Principle / Model
notes: In Q4, x is the remaining rectangle height after subtracting the triangle’s height.

title: Solve for Unknown in Area Equation
description: Substitute known values into an area formula and solve for the unknown dimension.
condition: An area formula with one unknown variable and known numerical values.
response: Set up an equation and solve algebraically for the unknown.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given $864 = \tfrac{1}{2}(x+8)(24)$, solve to find $x=32$.

title: Subtract Lengths to Find Remaining Segment
description: Find an unknown segment by subtracting a known part from a total length.
condition: Total length T and known part p given, remaining length unknown.
response: Compute remaining length as $T - p$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If total height is 10 and triangle height is 6, then $x = 10 - 6 = 4$.

title: Sum Volumes of Composite Prisms
description: Add volumes of multiple rectangular prisms to find the total volume of a composite solid.
condition: Given volumes of separate rectangular prism sections forming one solid.
response: Add the individual volumes.
label: Principle / Model
example: If chocolate sections total $1{,}152$ in³ and strawberry section is $480$ in³, then total volume $= 1{,}152 + 480 = 1{,}632$ in³.
notes: Applies additive property of volume.

title: Surface Area of Rectangular Prism
description: Compute the surface area of a rectangular prism by summing the areas of its faces (excluding any open faces if specified).
condition: Dimensions $l,w,h$ of a rectangular prism are known; determine area of all exterior faces (possibly excluding top).
response: Compute sum of areas of each face, e.g., $2lw + 2lh + 2wh$ (adjust if a face is missing).
label: Principle
example: For $l=2$, $w=1.5$, $h=1.5$ with open top, area $= lw + 2lh + 2wh = 3 + 6 + 6 = 15$.
notes: Exclude top face when stated. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Volume of a Rectangular Prism
description: Compute volume by multiplying length, width, and height.
condition: A rectangular prism with dimensions $l,w,h$.
response: Compute $V = lwh$.
label: Principle
notes: Applies to right rectangular prisms. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]
